Feature_Name: Inter-gNB-DU LTM Handover UE Context Setup Procedure

3GPP_Reference.Title: NG-RAN; F1 Application Protocol (F1AP), NG-RAN; Architecture description

Feature_Description: This procedure is used for the case when the UE moves from one gNB-DU to another gNB-DU within the same gNB-CU during NR operation for LTM (L1/L2 Triggered Mobility).

The purpose of the UE Context Setup procedure is to establish the UE Context including, among others, SRB, DRB, BH RLC channel, Uu Relay RLC channel, PC5 Relay RLC channel, and SL DRB configuration in the candidate gNB-DU for the LTM handover.
Feature_Objectives: To establish the UE Context in the candidate gNB-DU for an Inter-gNB-DU LTM handover.
To configure LTM-specific resources and parameters in the candidate gNB-DU.
To enable early synchronization and lower layer configuration for the LTM candidate cell.
To provide mapping information for LTM candidate cells.

Protocol_Interfaces: F1-C (Control Plane interface between gNB-CU and gNB-DU)

Feature_Implementation_Steps: 1. The gNB-CU determines to initiate LTM configuration based on L3 Measurement Reports from the UE.

2. The gNB-CU sends a UE CONTEXT SETUP REQUEST message to the candidate gNB-DU(s) for each candidate cell, containing one candidate cell ID and the CSI resource configuration for subsequent LTM. The gNB-CU may provide the LTM configuration ID mapping list to the candidate gNB-DU(s). The gNB-CU may request PRACH resources from the candidate gNB-DU(s). The gNB-CU may request the candidate gNB-DU(s) to provide the lower layer configuration for the purpose of generating the reference configuration or provide the lower layer part of the reference configuration to the candidate gNB-DU(s).

3. If the candidate gNB-DU accepts the request of LTM configuration, it responds with a UE CONTEXT SETUP RESPONSE message including the generated lower layer RRC configurations for the accepted target candidate cell.
Procedure_Implementation_Steps: 1. The gNB-CU initiates the procedure by sending a UE CONTEXT SETUP REQUEST message to the candidate gNB-DU.

2. If the LTM Indicator IE set to "true" is contained in the LTM Information Setup IE included in the UE CONTEXT SETUP REQUEST message, the gNB-DU shall, if supported, consider that the request concerns LTM for the included SpCell ID IE and shall include it as the Requested Target Cell ID IE in the UE CONTEXT SETUP RESPONSE message. The gNB-DU shall regard it as a reconfiguration with sync as defined in TS 38.331 [8].

3. If the Request for Lower Layer Configuration IE set to "true" is contained within the Reference Configuration IE in the LTM Information Setup IE included in the UE CONTEXT SETUP REQUEST message, the gNB-DU shall, if supported, provide the lower layer configuration in the Reference Configuration Information IE in the LTM Configuration IE in the UE CONTEXT SETUP RESPONSE message for the gNB-CU to generate the LTM reference configuration.

4. If the Reference Configuration Information IE is contained within the Reference Configuration IE in the LTM Information Setup IE included in the UE CONTEXT SETUP REQUEST message, the gNB-DU shall, if supported, take it into account for generating the LTM lower layer configuration.

5. If the CSI Resource Configuration IE is contained in the LTM Information Setup IE included in the UE CONTEXT SETUP REQUEST message, the gNB-DU shall, if supported, use it to generate the LTM CSI reporting configuration(s) in the CellGroupConfig IE for the requested LTM candidate cell.

6. If the LTM Configuration ID Mapping List IE is contained in the UE CONTEXT SETUP REQUEST message, the gNB-DU shall, if supported, consider this as the mapping information for the LTM candidate cell(s).

7. If the Early Sync Information Request IE is included in the UE CONTEXT SETUP REQUEST message, the gNB-DU shall, if supported, include the Early Sync Information IE of the accepted candidate cell for early TA acquisition (early UL synchronisation), in the UE CONTEXT SETUP RESPONSE message.

8. If the LTM Configuration IE is included in the UE CONTEXT SETUP RESPONSE message, the gNB-CU shall, if supported, consider it as the generated configuration for LTM from the accepted candidate cell in the candidate gNB-DU.

9. If the Complete Candidate Configuration Indicator IE set to "complete" is contained in the LTM Configuration IE included in the UE CONTEXT SETUP RESPONSE message, the gNB-CU shall, if supported, consider that the LTM candidate configuration is a complete candidate configuration.

10. If the gNB-DU is not able to establish an F1 UE context, or cannot even establish one bearer, it shall consider the procedure as failed and reply with the UE CONTEXT SETUP FAILURE message. If the LTM Indicator IE was included in the UE CONTEXT SETUP REQUEST message, the gNB-DU shall include the received SpCell ID IE as the Requested Target Cell ID IE in the UE CONTEXT SETUP FAILURE message.

Code_Instructions.description: Defines the structure of the F1AP UE Context Setup Request message, which the gNB-CU sends to the gNB-DU. This data structure is crucial for defining the format of the request message.
Defines the structure of the F1AP UE Context Setup Response message, which the gNB-DU sends back to the gNB-CU. This data structure is essential for defining the format of the response message.

A helper function used by the gNB-CU to generate the RRC-specific parts of the UE Context Setup Request message payload. This function prepares the RRC configuration that will be encapsulated within the F1AP message.

A utility function likely involved in the encoding or preparation of the F1AP UE Context Setup Request message. It facilitates the construction of the F1AP message from higher layer (RRC/MAC) information.

A utility function likely involved in the decoding or processing of the F1AP UE Context Setup Response message received by the gNB-CU, or for generating the response on the gNB-DU side. It helps in handling the F1AP response message content.

Information_Elements.IE_Definition: UEContextSetupRequestIEs F1AP-PROTOCOL-IES ::= { ID id-LTMInformation-Setup ... | ID id-LTMConfigurationIDMappingList ... | ID id-EarlySyncInformationRequest ... | ID id-SpCellID ... | ... }
UEContextSetupResponseIEs F1AP-PROTOCOL-IES ::= { ID id-RequestedTargetCellID ... | ID id-LTMConfiguration ... | ID id-EarlySyncInformation ... | ... }

LTMConfiguration ::= SEQUENCE {
 sSBInformation     SSBInformation,
 referenceConfigurationInformation  ReferenceConfigurationInformation   OPTIONAL,
 completeCandidateConfigurationIndicator  CompleteCandidateConfigurationIndicator     OPTIONAL,
 lTMCFRAResourceConfig    LTMCFRAResourceConfig      OPTIONAL,
 lTMCFRAResourceConfigSUL   LTMCFRAResourceConfig      OPTIONAL,
 iE-Extensions      ProtocolExtensionContainer { { LTMConfiguration-ExtIEs } } OPTIONAL,
 ...
}

Piggy_Backed_IES_Info: The CellGroupConfig IE is transparently signaled to the UE as part of RRC Reconfiguration. The RRCReconfiguration message, which includes the LTM configuration, is sent to the UE via the DL RRC MESSAGE TRANSFER message. The UE responds with an RRCReconfigurationComplete message, which is forwarded to the gNB-CU via the UL RRC MESSAGE TRANSFER message.
